title: Energy is the per-turn card resource
summary: Energy is the resource used each turn to play cards. Most cards cost between 0 and 3 energy.

title: Block reduces incoming damage
summary: Block reduces incoming damage. According to the core guide, block is normally removed at the end of the turn.

title: Draw adds cards from the draw pile to hand
summary: Draw means taking cards from the draw pile into the hand. The general turn flow guide states the player draws 5 cards at the start of a turn.

title: X-cost cards spend all remaining energy
summary: The knowledge base states that X-cost cards consume all remaining energy when played.

title: Turn start grants energy and draws cards
summary: At turn start, start-of-turn effects resolve, the player gains energy, and 5 cards are drawn.

title: Turn end discards hand and clears block
summary: At turn end, end-of-turn effects resolve, the hand is discarded, and block resets to zero according to the current guide text.

title: Enemy intents preview the next action
summary: Enemies follow predefined action patterns and their intent icons can be used to predict whether the next action is attack, defense, buff, or debuff oriented.

title: Deck thinning is a core build principle
summary: The strategy guide recommends removing starter cards and keeping a smaller pool of higher-quality cards when possible.

title: Energy curve should balance low and high cost cards
summary: The strategy guide recommends balancing cheap and expensive cards so that turns remain playable and efficient.

title: A stable defensive source is required
summary: The general build principles explicitly recommend making sure the deck has a reliable source of block or other defense.

title: Defect uses an orb system with multiple orb types
summary: The overview guide states that Defect can have up to 3 active orbs at once, with lightning, frost, dark, and plasma as the listed orb types, and that passive orb effects matter.

title: Ironclad strength scaling rewards repeated attacks
summary: The Ironclad overview emphasizes that strength stacking increases attack damage and that multi-hit attacks gain especially high value from this scaling.

title: Silent poison deals repeat end-of-turn damage
summary: The Silent guide frames poison as a core plan that stacks damage over time, with Catalyst and Noxious Fumes highlighted as key cards in that strategy.
